value.author: codex-docs-agent-ux-review-20260607
value.text: Sub-agent docs/agent-UX review: reviewed README.md, AGENTS.md, docs/COMMANDS.md, docs/CONFIGURATION.md, docs/TESTING.md, docs/EXTENSIONS.md, and docs/SDK.md for progressive disclosure, stale examples, duplicated guidance, token-efficient output guidance, and agent blockers. Finding: no blocking stale command examples or broken requested-doc links found; command-surface spot checks matched current help/contracts for install/package/upgrade/comments/docs/plan. value.author: codex-sdk-review-20260607
value.text: Sub-agent SDK review: reviewed src/sdk/index.ts, src/sdk/testing.ts, src/sdk/runtime.ts, src/sdk/package-runtime-options.ts, src/sdk/package-import-adapters.ts, src/core/packages/manifest.ts, docs/SDK.md, docs/EXTENSIONS.md, package scaffold/docs, and focused SDK/package tests. Findings: (1) Agent contract snapshot drift: pnpm contracts:check fails because live init contracts include --force but tests/fixtures/contracts/full.json is missing it at line 2619; stable temp diff shows only the added init --force flag. Recommended fix: run pnpm contracts:update, review fixture diff, and include intentional changelog/pm evidence. (2) Low-risk package-author ergonomics/docs-code mismatch: src/cli/commands/extension/scaffold.ts:47-58 says package mode emits documented defineExtension SDK shape, but lines 59-78 always emit the import-free JSDoc/plain object shape and tests/unit/extension-command.spec.ts:224-227 explicitly assert no defineExtension import for package init. The scaffold works, but decide whether package init should be SDK-first defineExtension for richer package authoring or update the comment/docs to state package init intentionally stays dependency-light. Verification: npm install -g .; pm --version=2026.6.7; node v25.9.0; pnpm 10.33.4; pnpm build PASS; node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/sdk-index.spec.ts tests/unit/package-manifest.spec.ts tests/unit/package-runtime-options.spec.ts tests/unit/package-import-adapters.spec.ts tests/integration/package-command-discovery.integration.spec.ts PASS (5 files, 73 tests); temp project /tmp/pm-sdk-review-xHSSbT init/package-init/install/invoke/doctor PASS; runtime contract for full command path agent-workflow-package ping PASS; pm validate --check-resolution --check-history-drift ok with existing validate_resolution_missing_fields:89 warning; pnpm contracts:check FAIL as above. Changed files by this sub-agent: none.

value.author: codex-github-observability-review-20260607
value.text: Sub-agent GitHub/observability review: gh repo view confirmed unbraind/pm-cli public repo, admin viewer, default branch main. gh pr list found no open PRs. gh issue list found one open issue, #140 Clarify --path semantics for agents and add safe guardrails, which matches this work item. gh run list found latest main CI run 27087018491 on 9c77af88 failed; gh run view/log-failed isolated it to Gates static -> pnpm changelog:pm:check, with CHANGELOG.md out of date after latest tracker close/release state. Local pnpm changelog:pm:check reproduced the same failure. CodeQL latest main run 27087018500 succeeded; latest Nightly Validation 27086714005 succeeded. Security checks: gh vulnerability-alerts endpoint returned 204 enabled; dependabot/code-scanning/secret-scanning alert list endpoints returned 404 via gh API, so not usable as clean/dirty evidence. pnpm audit --audit-level moderate passed with no known vulnerabilities. pnpm security:scan passed with no credential-like secrets detected in tracked files. Observability: sentry-telemetry-gate passed in best-effort mode with live SENTRY_PERSONAL_ADMIN_TOKEN, critical=0, high=0, ignored_expected_cli_error_total=14, telemetry finish_error_rate_pct=2.07, missing error-code rows=0. scripts/prod/telemetry/check-stack-health.sh showed remote stack healthy, API version 2026.6.7, RabbitMQ pm.telemetry.events queue depth 0 consumers 1, Postgres 17099 events in last 24h. Workflow review: CI uses pinned actions, least contents:read permission, concurrency cancellation, full-history checkout for changelog/static gates; CodeQL has security-events:write; Dependabot weekly npm and GitHub Actions groups are configured. Local release-gate subset node scripts/release/run-gates.mjs --json --skip-dogfood --skip-compatibility --telemetry-mode best-effort ... failed at coverage because concurrent --path/--pm-path lane edits currently break tests/unit/bootstrap-args.spec.ts and tests/unit/completion-command.spec.ts; did not patch because this overlaps active implementation lane rather than a disjoint CI/docs fix. Changed files by this sub-agent: none; reverted incidental pm-changelog managed-extension timestamp side effect.

value.author: codex-external-package-smoke-20260607
value.text:
Sub-agent external package smoke: bootstrap and current external package smoke complete.

Commands/results:
- `npm install -g .` -> ok, up to date.
- `pm --version` -> 2026.6.7; `node -v` -> v25.9.0; `pnpm -v` -> 10.33.4.
- `pnpm build` -> passed.
- `pnpm smoke:external-packages -- --discover-only --limit 25` -> ok=true, discovered 17 packages: pm-changelog, pm-jira, pm-github, pm-csv, @unbrained/pm-web, pm-beads, pm-graph, pm-gantt-chart, pm-slack, pm-slack-standup, pm-todos, pm-linear, pm-ts-starter, pm-presets, pm-starter, pm-brief, pm-context.
- `npm view pm-changelog version dist-tags --json` -> latest 2026.6.7.
- `pnpm smoke:external-packages -- --limit 25 --timeout-ms 180000` -> ok=true, cli_version=2026.6.7, tested=17, failed=0. Each package installed into an isolated temp project and passed init, install, `package doctor --project --detail deep --trace`, and `contracts --runtime-only --availability-only` with activation_failure_count=0 and blocking_failure_count=0.
- Non-blocking doctor warnings from the successful full run: pm-todos reported `extension_command_handler_collision`; pm-ts-starter and pm-starter reported `extension_output_renderer_override_global` and `extension_output_service_override_global`. These did not block activation or runtime contracts.
- Project-local `node dist/cli.js package doctor --project --detail summary --json` -> status ok, warning_count=0, activation_failure_count=0, blocking_failure_count=0, active_total=1 for managed pm-changelog.
- Project-local `node dist/cli.js contracts --runtime-only --availability-only --json` -> `changelog-export` and `changelog-generate` are invocable extension actions from provider=extension.

Caveat: a duplicate focused rerun of pm-todos, pm-ts-starter, and pm-starter later timed out during installs after 180s each while other smoke/changelog npm install processes were active. Because the prior full discovered smoke had already passed those packages and active concurrent npm/package processes were visible, I am treating that rerun as environmental install contention evidence, not a package compatibility failure.

Changed files: none by this sub-agent. Source/doc changes currently visible in git status are from other lanes.
